programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with two couples who had high expectations and long wish lists. Amy and Takie were desperate to get off the rental merry-go-round and buy their first home in London. Meanwhile, in Wiltshire Vicky and Andy were trying to combine their search for a home and two businesses on a budget of £400,000, which the couple eventually reconsidered once the reality of the market became clear.

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: As Keith and Marj await the go-ahead to transform the chapel into a home, pottery studio and events space, they start ripping out the rot and pitch their plan to the local community.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In South Wales, Richard hosts at his pub, setting the bar high with terrific tartlets, sensational sea bass and crowd-pleasing cheesecake. Can he hit bullseye?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Carl and Liz host the weeks second visit at The Red House in Cambridgeshire. Their coaching inn with converted stable bedrooms leaves the BandBers with some positive first impressions - until one pair of guests give their drawers a good dusting down.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: On the Costa Blanca, Abi and Darrens boogie wonderland is hit by technical trouble. And Gordon and Shona finally take the reins of a diving business in Marbella.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Wendy and Pete are looking to buy their first home together but have very different ideas of where it should be. Will it be Great Yarmouth in Norfolk or the countryside of Almeria in Spain?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Jean Johansson catches up with Bev and Paul in Spain, who she helped in 2018, while Danni Menzies travels back to Brittany to revisit Jill and Paul, who are living the good life in their farmhouse on their five acres of land that they bought in 2017.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in County Durham and Wearside is personal trainer Eve Booth, who is concerned that her haggis and black pudding tower starter might not be to everyones taste.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second to host in Durham and Wearside is interior designer and gypsy wedding supplier Paula Thompson, whos hoping to bring a touch of sparkle with her black and bling themed evening.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day three in County Durham and Wearside and the host is 19-year-old languages student Donal Anand-Shaw. Donal goes all out with a black tie dinner party, complete with string quartet and a locally sourced scallop starter.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Twenty-eight-year-old debt collector Eleri Watson is a Welsh farmers daughter and a fierce Welsh patriot, and for her night she has plumped for a Welsh menu, Welsh ingredients, and Welsh dress code.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final host in County Durham and Wearside is cheeky businessman Ken Brown, with his `wear a wig themed evening.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Yvette and Agata look for their dream forever home near the centre of the action in the seaside town of Alvor on Portugals Algarve. Jasmine shows them the best of what their £300k budget can get them.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil return for a new series, and in London its double trouble for Kirstie as she helps 67 identical twins Matt and Chris Wickham, who are clubbing together to buy their first home. Their £350,000 is a healthy first-time buyer budget. Phil helps Sam Taylor and his fiancee Krystle Maher, who are desperate to move out of their small rented flat in Deptford and into a home of their own.

programme.title: Nandos vs KFC: Battle of the Brands
programme.desc: KFC and Nandos go head-to-head as Kate Quilton explores the battle for our taste buds in a fiery look at flavour, fandom and the billion-pound fight to win the chicken wars.

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less

programme.desc: Teal is having a lovely time in the flowers that are blooming all over the island. She brings a bunch to Abney but they make him sneeze. His unstoppable sneezes get bigger and bigger and funny things start to happen.

programme.title: Daolag-bhreac is Seillean
programme.desc: Ladybird and Bee discover how strong ants really are.

programme.title: Oscar and Ealasaid
programme.desc: While out picking blackberries, Celestine finds an old mill covered in ivy. Ernest claims that the building is haunted. Instead of scaring Celestine, it piques her curiosity.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Fiona MacKenzie reads `Trì Muncaidhean Beaga by Quentin Blake, illustrated by Emma Chichester Clark and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Following an incident with the school photocopier, Dave must round up a horde of photocopied baby Spud twins.

programme.title: Hilda
programme.desc: There is something mysterious about the new girl on the street. And what does she have to do with Daibhidhs nightmares? Hilda wants to help him get over his fear of night-time.

programme.title: Naidheachdan Neoannach CBBC ALBA
programme.desc: Calum Macdonald introduces his personal highlights from the craziest news show on BBC ALBA!

programme.title: An Sgoil
programme.desc: No one is following Ciorstaidhs new systems, and she isnt happy about it.

programme.title: Golf: Mun Cuairt Alba
programme.desc: Those story of blind golfers who play for Scotland, and following European Tour player Laura Murray through more of her training.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: North Uist native and learner John Gillies chats to Joy, and a hungry Calum Maclean is on a quest for a mouth-watering cheese course at a family dairy in Easter Ross.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Mas Fhior
programme.desc: Niall Iain Macdonald hosts a quiz show where two teams go head-to-head to win a cash prize. Niall Iain Dòmhnallach le farpais cheist ùr is dà sgioba a strì airson duais airgid.

programme.title: An t-Uisge/Rain Stories
programme.desc: Rain has influenced our cultures and traditions since time began. Anne hears about acid rain, first identified after the Industrial Revolution, and visits a research site near Edinburgh where scientists monitor nitrogen pollution in our rain today.

programme.title: Trusadh
programme.desc: Turas fèin-aithne dha boireannach Dhubh tar-ghnèitheach ann am Brasil. A journey of self-discovery for a black trans woman in Brazil.

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: An seinneadair aithnichte Albannach Rianne Downey le seisean acoustic aig Belladrum 2025. Acclaimed Scottish singer Rianne Downey performs an acoustic session at Belladrum.

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Drama Gaidhlig steidhichte ann an Leodhas. Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Writer Stephen Smallberg is experiencing a dry spell. One night he gets inspiration in a dream and on waking rushes to commit the words to paper. Unfortunately a seagull and a goat also have their eyes on this tasty morsel.

programme.title: Alleluia
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon reads excerpts from Gliocas bhon Bhìoball by Donald Saunders and Mairi MacInnes narrates from The Mothers Catechism.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Janina is in Turkey, tracking a young explorer who found the worlds oldest city and rewrote the beginning of civilisation, until his reputation was destroyed by scandal.

programme.title: This Is BBC Four
programme.desc: BBC FOUR is the BBC channel for people who want more. More depth, more range, more to stimulate the mind.

programme.title: This Is BBC Four
programme.desc: BBC FOUR is the BBC channel for people who want more. More depth, more range, more to stimulate the mind.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers how the health and fortune of the village was shaped by those in charge of Bamburghs famous castle.

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia starts this walk in Birmingham, which surprisingly boasts more canals than Venice. She discovers how the city, through its canal network, became the epicentre of the Industrial Revolution. Its also the start of Julias two-day walk along the historic and picturesque Worcester and Birmingham Canal, which cuts a 30-mile path through to the River Severn.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is at her wits end. Theres the charity shop, Councillor Nugent, Roses love life - to say nothing of Daddys! Can she cope and keep the flag flying, let alone her sanity?

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy finds himself in trouble when the insanely jealous `Tiny Heseltine expresses an interest in Vanessa.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: In nine short hours, William the Conqueror triumphed at the Battle of Hastings - and England was changed forever. Simon Schama recounts the saga of blood, betrayal and ambition that led up to this pivotal battle and describes the profound consequences that followed.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A Storyville documentary that explores an increasingly popular phenomenon in contemporary China. A woman hires a mistress dispeller to bring her husbands affair to an end.

programme.title: Border Country: The Story of Britains Lost Middleland
programme.desc: Hadrians Wall cut a deep scar across Britain that would never be forgotten. A thousand years after the Romans left, the island split once again, near the line of the wall, into the Kingdoms of England and Scotland. Historian and MP, Rory Stewart tells the story of how Britain was torn in two. The border country dividing Britains lost Middleland became a zone of anarchy, as violent as border areas in the Balkans, Iraq and Afghanistan.

programme.title: Bridget Riley: Painting the Line
programme.desc: The story of a true visionary of British art. Bridget Riley has been challenging our perception through painting for over 60 years, with radical work that has transformed how we look at art and invites us to feel with our eyes.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers how the health and fortune of the village was shaped by those in charge of Bamburghs famous castle.

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia starts this walk in Birmingham, which surprisingly boasts more canals than Venice. She discovers how the city, through its canal network, became the epicentre of the Industrial Revolution. Its also the start of Julias two-day walk along the historic and picturesque Worcester and Birmingham Canal, which cuts a 30-mile path through to the River Severn.

programme.desc: Mathematician Professor Marcus du Sautoy demystifies the hidden world of algorithms.

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: Today, trainee harbour pilot Ralph Greig faces a tough test on the simulator on his first day of training. Meanwhile, ex-sailor Ellen Haughland has her heart set on stepping into Ralphs shoes as a VTS controller - can she become Aberdeens first ever woman in that role? Onboard the Bibby Sapphire, the crew brace themselves for a force ten storm heading directly towards them.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: A quad bike accident reveals the dangers of hill farming.

programme.title: Dunblane: How Britain Banned Handguns
programme.desc: Parents, politicians and campaigners tell the story of their fight to ban handguns after the shooting in which 16 children and a teacher were murdered with legal firearms.

programme.title: Scotland: The Promised Land
programme.desc: In the wake of the Great War, two visions of Scotland emerged - a nationalist cultural resurgence led by a band of writers, and a popular revival that appealed to the masses. This final episode examines the struggle between these two ideologies, charting the remarkable transformation of those who helped give birth to the modern Scotland of today.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotlands biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: David Wilsons Crime Files
programme.desc: Criminologist David Wilson probes Scotlands battle with terrorism and discovers how the problem is a lot closer to home than might be imagined. Investigative journalist Fiona Walker heads to Glasgow Airport, the site of one of Scotlands most notorious terrorist attacks. She recounts the events of 30 June 2007 which saw two men drove their jeep into the terminal building.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives some terrible news that reminds him to live life to its fullest. Will he manage the biggest physical challenge yet?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommys road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldnt train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity: a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi, and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to.

programme.title: Alan Carrs Picture Slam
programme.desc: This week, Alan puts three new teams through their picture paces: married couple Nic and Corin from Essex, father-son duo Alex and David from Aberdeenshire and Glasgow and friends Abbie and Maddy from Durham. Theyll be pitted against each other across four fun and furious rounds against the clock - including the brand new Picture Twist and buzzer rounds - with one pair making it to the final Super Slam. Will they be able to tell their kangaroo from their Doctor Who and take home the jackpot?

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The queens give drag makeovers to Team GB Olympians Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia guest-judges.

programme.title: Cat Person
programme.desc: A college student who works at a movie theatre goes on an awkward date with an older man who may be a murderer.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: When a mysterious doctor starts flirting with a former beauty queen online, she smells a love rat. But when she starts digging, the mystery deepens, and nothing is as it seems.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The women work together to reveal the true identity of their catfish. However, as more victims and evidence come to light, the women are forced to take matters into their own hands.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: With the perpetrator now investigated by the police as a serial offender, questions turn to what drove them to become one of the UKs prolific catfish - and why cant they stop?

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Bear Grylls - Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Married couple Steve and Jenny need to buy their first home together but cant agree on what they want. Will one convince the other, or can they compromise?

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Dom and Rich restore a storm-worn memorial in Kirkcudbright before a Remembrance service, and Will learns why Lyndsey is at her wits end trying to clean her prized cast iron pan.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The show is in Staffordshire, home of the world-renowned Potteries. Christina Trevanion meets a man with more than 1,200 Staffordshire figures, which he stores in a specially built room on the side of his house! Meanwhile, two teams go hunting for antiques at Bingley Hall in the company of their experts, Izzie Balmer and Nick Hall. In the red jackets are married couple Agnes and Irfan, and in blue are colleagues Lorraine and Hannah.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Gladiators Ready! Bradley and Barney Walsh welcome back four more steel-nerved Contenders to battle the indomitable Gladiators as they compete for the last semi-final places.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn invites a panel of guests to share their opinions on the newsmaking stories of the day.

programme.title: !mpossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: This walk takes Derek to the remote Berwyn Mountains, where he walks from Llangynog to the spectacular Pistyll Rhaeadr waterfall, one of the seven wonders of Wales.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earths Mythical Islands
programme.desc: The most extreme and wild parts of New Zealand are in the South Island, which lie towards Antarctica, in the path of the tempestuous `roaring forties. This is home to some of the most rapidly rising mountains in the world, the Southern Alps. From hyper-intelligent parrots, to sinister snails with teeth and magical constellations of glow-worms, this is the story of New Zealands wildest places and its most resilient pioneers, all of who must embrace radical solutions to survive.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A visit to Barnstaple in North Devon turns up an important enamel miniature by Henry Bone, a valuable collection of walking sticks, and a World War I pilots watch once worn by T E Lawrence.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Sheffield, where he is joined by antiques experts Thomas Plant and James Lewis as they search for interesting and valuable items to take to auction. Among their finds are a tortoiseshell locket, a Georgian tankard and an electrotherapy kit.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Champions.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: In Taunton, Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook on unbelievable facts and surprising stories.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for central England with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts travels across the Midlands, looking at the best archaeology uncovered last year in the heart of England. We join palaeontologist Dr Dean Lomax in the middle of the largest artificial lake in the country, Rutland Water.

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya is in Germanys Bavarian mountains, paragliding over Neuschwanstein Castle. Heading to Spain, Katya begins in the region of Catalonia, where she witnesses an extraordinary local custom.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts a very varied episode, joined by panellists Ross Noble, Sara Pascoe, Sally Phillips and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the days top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Multiple cameras follow serious crime investigations in real time, revealing the crucial role cutting edge forensic science now plays in bringing criminals to justice.

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: Writer and comedian Sara Pascoe returns with her unique travelogue learning how to do the worlds endangered jobs. In Denmark, she learns how to build from a Lego master and make a roof out of seaweed with a group of thatchers.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates head to Egypt to put on corporate away days for paying clients. One team embarks on a desert adventure but ends up having a bumpy ride, while the rest set off on a lagoon tour which is anything but relaxing. Food service proves problematic, and for one candidate, theres a one-way ticket home.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Together with the weeks newly-fired candidate, Angela Scanlon and her panel will dissect all the drama from that weeks task, providing fans with in-depth analysis and behind the scenes intel.

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: Freya and Jo revisit the past. Abes stage proposal doesnt go according to plan.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus time in Saint-Remy is nearly at an end. During his trip, hes been a sous-chef, been run over by goats, searched for snails, steered a combine harvester, and met some passionate producers. Now, hes been invited to a local farm party.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Bear Grylls - Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Former best friends Rhys and Niall work to repair their broken friendship. They drifted apart after the death of Nialls older brother and have barely spoken for two years.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Will and Dan get a model of the worlds only rotating boat lift back up and running, and Dom collects a precious toy that saw its owner through pioneering pre-natal treatment.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Scotlands Central Lowlands, Sonali Shah helps a Canadian couple to make a rural escape and meets Californians who bought their dream home, sight unseen, and never looked back.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: With the dominant males gone, trouble looms as intruder lions close in on the Xudum prides young cubs. Leopard Lediba searches for her missing cub after a tense encounter, and Pobe the cheetah must defend her familys food from bigger cats.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn invites a panel of guests to share their opinions on the newsmaking stories of the day.

programme.title: !mpossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to Llandudno but he is not there to hit the beach, he is off to explore the rugged beauty of Great Orme, a giant headland overlooking the Irish Sea. Along the way he visits St Tudnos church and copper mines that date back four thousand years.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earths Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand was one of the last land-masses to be found and settled by people. Lush and fertile, almost everything brought here flourishes, often with surprising consequences. Told through the experiences of its native species - in particular, a charismatic and peculiar giant, flightless parrot - this is the moving story of the changing fortunes of New Zealands wildlife, since humans first arrived.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. This episode was filmed in the Valley Leisure Centre in Biddulph, Staffordshire.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by antique experts David Barby and Phillip Serrell at The Council House in Bristol. They find a gold watch with a mysterious history and a collection of medals that leaves the owner unsure whether she wants to sell. Paul explores the legacy of Bristols adopted son Brunel, with a visit to Temple Meads station and Clifton suspension bridge.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Champions.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The tour rolls into Paisley as Vernon Kay and unreliable guide Paul Gorton see if Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook can separate the real from the ridiculous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from central England compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: After eight years in build HMS Queen Elizabeth embarks on dangerous sea trials in the North Sea. The 700 sailors on board have to test everything on the ship for the first time, from galleys to guns and from power plants to propellers.

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: The kidnapping of John and James sparks a perilous manhunt as Islamic State emerges, more journalists join them in captivity and survival becomes a desperate struggle.

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: A benefits assessor comes to evaluate Freyas needs and determine how many hours of PA financial assistance shell receive. Jo unexpectedly turns up at the venue for Janes book launch to try to make some progress with a clearly wounded Levi.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the days top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: John Simpson brings his expertise to bear on the most important stories. In discussion with the BBCs range of experts across the world, he uses their unique local knowledge to go beyond the headlines to get to the unspun truth about the world.

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh visits Sierra Leone, a country with a dark past but great hopes for the future. With musician Gwyn Jay Allen as a guide, he explores its national parks and meets locals living truly off-grid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for central England with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya visits Italy and Germany to explore how theyre changing in the turbulent new world. She attends the Palio in Siena, joins a Nato exercise and meets a family building a nuclear bunker.

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Examining two pivotal cases from the driverless car revolution, including the first death of a pedestrian - who should be held responsible?

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: On the Eve of the Salmon Season - The River Tweed
programme.desc: With the new salmon-fishing season about to open on the River Tweed, Sammi Kinghorn and Matt Baker follow the final preparations before the first cast of the year.

programme.title: Swamp People
programme.desc: King of the Swamp Troy Landry gambles on Terrals secret honey hole, while Willie gives his son the reins for the first time and Ronnie baits with rotten duck.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Pickle must face her fear of big cats as she continues to impress Troy with her natural snake hunting talent. Bill and Tes hunt stumble on a rumoured government testing facility and find all they can handle.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Follow the quirky day-to day operations of American Jewelry and Loan, a family-owned and operated pawn shop in Detroit, Michigan.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of antique dealers travel to Stockport to give members of the public the chance to put their valuables into auction or accept a cash sum. Brenda Haller encounters a stationary box worth writing home about; a maritime picture leaves David Hakeney awash with wonder, but its a pocket watch and set of sovereigns that turns the den into a gold mine today.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his antique dealers travel to Welwyn Garden City, where an unusual letter opener and some flying helmets are amongst the items brought in, also of interest is a beautiful plaque of a lady by famous maker KPN and a gentlemans vanity set.

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: Scott decides to sell the entire collection of treasures in his man cave.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and his partners in grime Allen Millyard and Guy Willison hit the road once again in their relentless hunt for automotive treasures - this time they visit renowned collector Francis and secure a vintage Kawasaki at the National Motorcycle Museums autumn auction. They head to a mega auto-jumble at Kempton racecourse with over two thousand pounds worth of stock.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy drags Isaiah to Victorville as punishment for getting Cs on his report card. Kenny wants to furnish his new house, but there are eight reasons standing in his way. And not to be cheesy, but Lisa learns a lot.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Brandi is fired up about expanding her brand in San Bernardino. Rene is positive that everyone is out to get him. Dusty tackles a locker dustier than him. Kenny is a mess.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth tries to make amends and Les checks out an old school peep show. Ashley helps a customer overcome his vocal issue.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A major miscommunication between Les and Seth could land an employee on the chopping block; Ashley tries to keep the peace.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: When Rick gets his hands on a pretty stellar coin, Corey flips about the astronomical asking price. Next, Chum goes to the mat when an NWA Championship Wrestling Belt elbows its way into the shop.

programme.title: Swamp People
programme.desc: Jacob leads the Landry clan into Big Bull Bayou; Chase hunts secluded channels with his tail boat; Daniel and Big Tee track a giant cannibal; Joey hunts solo.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Troy and Pickle hunt a giant python known as the Night Serpent; Tess convinces Bill to try out new hunting grounds; Zak and Aaron explore a notorious corner of the Everglades; Dusty Crum has an idea to lighten his load.

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: Scott decides to sell the entire collection of treasures in his man cave.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Lisa returns to Moreno Valley, where it all started, and gets nostalgic with her locker. Kenny welcomes a new granddaughter to the clan and would love to find an appropriate unit. Dusty and Lupe put a little elbow grease into their purchase.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy takes on a supervisory role in Stanton and it leads him to a bit of Slavic success. Brandi tries to help Danielle outfit her new apartment. Lisa gambles on a painting.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and top restorers Allen Millyard and Guy Willison scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other peoples automotive junk and classic collectibles.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The discovery of the ancient megalithic site of Karahan Tepe in southern Turkey turned the world of archaeology on its head, displaying engineering skills far beyond what the people of the time were thought to be capable of.

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained
programme.desc: A mysterious opening on the Red Planet caught on camera, a UFO appears to invade an Amazon village, and an unidentified spacecraft disrupts the queens jubilee celebrations in London in June 2022.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Chum chases down a lead on a 1963 Fiat Microcar. Next, Jake gets a lesson from big brother Corey when a rare musical instrument enters the shop. Rick and Corey disagree on a set of billiard balls.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy drags Isaiah to Victorville as punishment for getting Cs on his report card. Kenny wants to furnish his new house, but there are eight reasons standing in his way. And not to be cheesy, but Lisa learns a lot.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Brandi is fired up about expanding her brand in San Bernardino. Rene is positive that everyone is out to get him. Dusty tackles a locker dustier than him. Kenny is a mess.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and top restorers Allen Millyard and Guy Willison scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other peoples automotive junk and classic collectibles.

programme.title: UFO: POV
programme.desc: Inside the dreaded Alaskan Triangle the location of over 20,000 disappearances over the past 50 years, 4 residents of Yakutat, Alaska have an encounter that they wont soon forget when they get up close and personal with the unexplained in December 2019. The exoplanet LHS 1140 b is used to base our experts theories as to who these visitors may be and what their POV may be like.

programme.title: Roswell 75: The Final Evidence
programme.desc: This July, 75 years have passed since the Roswell incident. This two-part documentary revisits the alleged crash of an extra terrestrial craft at Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Tired of eating grass, Shaun sets out to pinch one of the Farmers prized apples only to have every attempt ruined by his constantly sneezing accomplice - a sheep, who much to Shauns frustration, has decided to be his new best friend.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: In order to stop a deranged Victorian time-travelling chimp from un-inventing the world, Danger Mouse and Penfold must arrive in the past just before the nick of time.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes quest under the sea for bath sponges in Merhaven.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Girl enters a cosplay costume contest to win a trophy and meet her favourite TV star, but needs somebody to wear the back end of her enormous costume. Boy is horrified at dressing as the characters bum, but he doesnt want to let his sister down. He puts aside his embarrassment to help Girl win the competition.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a hidden mechanism on the Great Wall of China.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Sandy have to collect Krabby patty ingredients, but Plankton is following them.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Olive and Otto get trapped inside a board game.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: With Joy having been let down by the builders, Danny and Mick step up and offer to build the leisure centres new sauna themselves. But can they follow flat-pack instructions? And will their antics get Joy hot and bothered?

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Explore what would happen if it was possible to build a slide to the other side of the world. You would need to start in space, use the slippiest slide ever and make sure you have enough supplies before you go down it.

programme.title: Bitesize: 7-9 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about solids, liquids and gas, heating and cooling and the water cycle.

programme.title: Blow Your Mind
programme.desc: Dr Chris and Dr Xand learn about meteorites and what happens when they land on Earth.

programme.title: After Life: Rot Box Detectives
programme.desc: A team of young science detectives investigate rot and decay through a series of experiments and activities, assisted by Dr George McGavin.

programme.title: Shark Bites
programme.desc: Steve Backshall looks at gentle giant the basking shark, a huge shark found in the UK. For such a large shark, it has a somewhat surprising diet. It visits Britains coastal waters every spring and summer. Steve heads to his shark lab to reveal why.

programme.title: Operation Ouch! Hospital Takeover
programme.desc: The doctors reveal how your eardrum works to enable you to hear and they find out how tough it is to be hospital radio hosts in Operation Takeover. Dr Chris discovers what it takes for anesthetists to keep you safe and pain-free during operations, and Ouch and About On Call hits the wards to answer patients medical questions. Meanwhile, in Accident and Emergency, one patient comes in with a cut to his hand, and another is treated for a football injury.

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: Big Danny and Big Manny find science shocking, Da Vinci does a sound experiment, Newton rides again, Queen Vic shows off, and Dr Big Brain doesnt see the attraction of electromagnets!

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.sub-title: You Could Run at the Speed of Sound?
programme.desc: A fun run goes wrong when one ambitious runner tries running at the speed of sound.

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Zoe from Oxfordshire is mad about science and dreams of becoming a scientist one day. She loves to carry out messy, noisy experiments, but her dad works night shifts, which means she has to be quiet while he sleeps. She desperately wants her own space where she can pursue her passions and make a mess whenever she wants!

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Helena is 11 and an only child. She loves star-gazing, wants to be an astrophysicist when shes older and is passionate about girls getting into science. She lives above her mum and dads bookshop where they often work seven days a week which means she doesnt get the chance to get out and look at the night sky! Helena needs a den to help her reach for the stars and fulfil her science dreams.

programme.title: Andy And The Band
programme.desc: Odd Sock Aniya needs to break a Planet Record TODAY! Andy and the Band wont give up - even with Whiff trying to stop them.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Giant is pranked by Nitro when he appears as a guest of honour at a fake school award ceremony! The other Gladiators have sneaky fun backstage at Sheffield Arena.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.sub-title: Octonauts and the Baby Alligator Search
programme.desc: A baby alligator is adopted, but when it escapes from the Octopod, the team must rescue the tiny creature from ocean currents and hungry predators.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: When Cosima finally finds a quiet place to read her book, she realises peace and quiet arent all theyre made out to be. Wheres Big Lizard when she needs him?

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.sub-title: Five Piris and a Little Egg
programme.desc: The Piris agree to mind a package for Shirley but discover its a precious egg! Watching over Eggy turns out to be exhausting, but then Shirley returns with unexpected news.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes must urgently quest to Mojo Mountain and speak to the Silver Faun in order to prevent Hoofius from losing his mojo forever.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest

programme.desc: A rocket carries the cabin into space.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dynamite sets Drs Dan and Raph an epic Body Battles task, and Billy becomes a mindbender.

programme.title: Armorsaurs
programme.desc: Helmsworth demands answers from Switchblade.

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.desc: Dani Harmer promotes the new series of The Dumping Ground... and its chaos!

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel. Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Tish Tash
programme.desc: Pola gifts Tish a magic set and challenges her to learn at least one trick by the end of the day, to which she excitedly agrees.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing builds a house out of the sofa cushions, and Coco decides its the perfect starting point for a game of Big Bad Wolf with Sula and Charlie. Bing enjoys being Wolfie until hes not allowed into the house he built.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun moving backwards and forwards. The Tubby Custard Ride goes forwards, then backwards, then forwards again.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee enjoys his time in the paddling pool and doesnt want to get out. Can Mo lure him out of it with the promise of a warm bath?

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea takes all the books from the supermarket. Supertato, Chilli and Carrot try to rescue them by using acrobatic skills that they learnt from their favourite circus book.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are painting in the garden. When Bing cleans his brush and accidentally knocks the water over, he goes to fill it up from the garden tap. But he turns the water on too fast, making the hose voosh up into a big snake of water that covers Bing and Sula in wet paint! Flop shows Bing how to turn the tap on slowly.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Its secret agent time when Mojo and Bo think Didi the tractor is up to something fishy. But can these two supersecret spies follow her without being spy-spotted?

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Spring has sprung! There are cute baby animals to meet and frolic with, Hennie bounces around in new dance shoes, and Happy loves water as much as Roly loves potatoes!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tino invites everyone to enter his summer exhibition competition but the Squirrels are not sure what to create. Duggee suggest to paint something they know and love.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Dads accused of fluffing in Blueys face as he climbs out of bed, hes put on trial with Mum as the judge, and Bluey and Bingo as witnesses. Bluey might also have to explain what she was doing there.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Uncle Rad and Frisky accidentally turn up to babysit Bluey and Bingo on the same night, they have to work together to make Bluey feel better about babysitter put-downs.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.sub-title: Mountain Gorillas, The Democratic Republic of the Congo
programme.desc: When a cold-ridden Glitchs sneezing breaks his TV, the Grimbots take him to the Virunga National Park in the Democratic Republic of the Congo to see a real mountain gorilla. But Glitch doesnt realise how dangerous his cold germs can be, so the Go Jetters will have to stop him from making a baby gorilla sick - all while trying to keep it safe from dangerous traps left by hunters.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Bird Trap
programme.desc: When Mr Tods efforts to creep up on the rabbits are thwarted by a noisy song thrush, he cages the bird so that he can ambush Peter and his friends without warning.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: On a camping trip, the Numberblocks need to work out how to share things fairly.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. From A to Z, every Alphablock has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. S, T, O and P get caught on a runaway bus, what can they do?

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has to deal with being the only monster in a town full of cute baby animals. Based on the `Love Monster series of books by award-winning British author and illustrator Rachel Bright.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella plays with her favourite cup - it makes the perfect drum for a sing along with Lift. Together, they visit Kingsley and Queenie who play the Tea Party Dash, but who will win?

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its the fabulous Avocados singing an operatic piece in their three unique voices!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing gets jealous when Sula pays more attention to Pando on a play date. As he feels more and more left out, Bing tries to get Sulas attention and spoils Sulas tea party. Sula pushes Bing and they both end up angry. Amma shows them a way to get rid of all their anger so the toy party can continue.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `zebra, and its time to go on a whirlwind word adventure to meet a striped zebra, a spiky hair zebra, a lazing zebra, a zooming zebra and a baby zebra.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu upsets the other bugs when she refuses to share the funnest role in a game.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Fiona has to babysit the Bunnies, but theyre bouncing off the walls! She calls the Piggies for help, but even they find this task impossible.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: An artist is drawing a dog in a room. The dog wants to go out, so the artist opens the door. After a while, the artist opens the door again and calls the dog back inside. The Twirlywoos decide to open and close the door to let the dog in and out.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin visits a friends school to make a recycled dragon and to take part in a dragon parade. Meanwhile, its school photo day with Teacher Tumble - say cheese!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play with a bubble and then Po and Laa-Laa play catch with a ball. In Tummy Tales, two brothers learn to catch a ball and play catch with their dog.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Amber, Amelia, Arthur, Ezra, Luke, Willow, Xander and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyones bag in this episode are hats which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding an appropriate hat inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The nursery rhyme Dingle Dangle Scarecrow features, as does a story involving Baby Bear.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee joins her friend Karlaine to watch a rugby match and play a game of I Spy.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Its Novah and Nellys turn to accompany Aaron on a unique garden adventure. Today, the team will need to be all ears as they take in the sounds their city centre plot has to share.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.desc: Dylan the deep-sea diver wants to take a picture of the deepest darkest bottom of the sea, but when he discovers its too dark for his camera, he turns to a new friend.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The gang sing a guitar driven, rock and roll anthem celebrating their feelings.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas reluctantly babysit a mischievous baby Bananaraptor that proves to be more than a handful.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad chides Bluey for not minding her manners at the dinner table, but when Bluey gets a hold of a magical asparagus stick, she proves that everyone has a wild side.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are excited to go to a black-and-white gala at King Tigers house. On arrival, theyre impressed with King Tigers party crown and its resplendent red ruby centrepiece, even if his composure is somewhat ruined by frequent and giant sneezes. The Squirrels set about testing out Duggees golden rules for party etiquette by mingling with fellow guests but get a suspiciously lukewarm response.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Catchers land on planet Cartoon and make friends with two cute alien kids: Toby and Penny. Their favourite game is Shoot the Can.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy is fascinated by the Moonscape collage that he and his friends make - especially the little green aliens that live on the moon. Finlay notices this, and with Harriet and Osbournes help, he gets his friends to trick Timmy into thinking that aliens have landed at the nursery.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Amanpreet and Mum make delicious pakora together for a special family meal to celebrate Vaisakhi. They set the table and exchange beautiful Vaisakhi cards with Dad, but what will everyone think of Amanpreets fabulous feast?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Emilios Cypriot Pitta Bread with Halloumi
programme.desc: Emilios family are from Cyprus, an island nation in the Mediterranean Sea. Emilio is making two Cypriot recipes for his friends to enjoy. The first is pitta bread! The dough is formed and rolled into ovals and placed on a baking tray.

programme.title: Ferne and Rorys Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Rory and Sandy are searching for millipedes while Chantelle meets a colourful creature. Find out how they help the planet and try this Teeny Tiny Task - recycling like millipedes.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: When Dans favourite toy goes missing at the Search and Rescue Centre Open Day, Dan needs his super nose to race to the rescue of Squiddy!

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Coquerels Sifaka and Vampire Crab
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet Coquerels sifakas and vampire crabs.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells take Waffle to school to ask if he can visit class. When Headteacher, Mr Nolan, says no, Waffle goes missing, hes determined to prove hes ready for school.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Bernie needs a new shell. Oona and Silky find him the perfect replacement.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes worries that the Sundial Stage isnt loud enough when his guitar hero comes to play a gig. With Yukees help, Stripes is determined to turn the volume up to 11, but is that too loud?

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Chloe is busy painting a picture of herself and Topsy. Topsy is bored waiting for her to finish, and tries to help.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the squirrels upset the mole by being noisy, Duggee suggests ways that they can all live underground peacefully.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say hat. See lots of different hats including a small hat, a big hat and even a woolly pink pig hat! Theres a pretty flower hat and a funny tower hat. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Meet the Six Times Table, shes the table of Sixes. If youre stuck on Six, shes got tricks and quick fixes! She loves to rhyme about all the Numberblocks that are part of her team. Most Numberblocks are familiar faces, such as Six, Twelve, Eighteen, Twenty-Four, Thirty, Thirty-Six and Sixty.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Its talent night and A and R are trying to do some magic, but their card trick keeps going wrong.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin has lots of outdoor summer fun with his friends. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble judges the Best Kept Garden competition. Will the award go to Aunt Polly or Grandad Tumble?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies take selfies with the Tubby Phone and a child in Tummy Tales has their photo taken in a photo booth with their grandparent. Then its time for a Tubby Phone Dance.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Tiger has a problem: he wants to play Boo, but the Patchwork Pals dont want to. Will Tiger find someone to play with?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.sub-title: Raa Raa and the Speedy Racers
programme.desc: Raa Raa and his friends are racing their vehicles through the Jingly Jangly Jungle, but who will win?

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Tai must learn how to remain focused, calm and balanced while on the job.

programme.title: Gigglebiz

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace steers a four-wheel all-terrain vehicle, shows off a huge mining truck and then test drives a tracked all-terrain vehicle.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers are five friends who travel through space exploring fantastic planets with one big mission: to discover, play and collect all the games in the universe.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When a floating garbage patch endangers a flock of pelicans and other sea creatures, the Octonauts and the birds work together to clean it up.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Cat and the Rat
programme.desc: When Jemima and the ducklings are driven from their coop by Sammy Whiskers, Peter must enlist the unwitting help of Mr. McGregors Cat to get her home back.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are playing at the clubhouse when there is a knock at the door. Its delivery Chipmunk with a special delivery for Duggee and the Squirrels. Duggee opens the envelope and takes out the card inside: It is with very great pleasure that I invite Duggee and the Squirrels to my wedding. King Tiger has met his perfect partner and is getting married. Its going to be a royal wedding!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato and Evil Pea go head-to-head on a game show to try and win a speedboat.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Ducklings cook up some lovely vegetable dishes, but Weasy will only eat his popcorn.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: When a lost bird takes a liking to Bo, it is up to Mojo to fly it to its home. But its a stormy trip, and Bo will need to do some flying of his own!

programme.desc: When Dads accused of fluffing in Blueys face as he climbs out of bed, hes put on trial with Mum as the judge, and Bluey and Bingo as witnesses. Bluey might also have to explain what she was doing there.

programme.desc: A 10-year-old calls 911: he shot his dad. It seems like a tragic accident-until nine years later, he reveals a darker truth.

programme.title: Spring Break Murders
programme.desc: Revisiting homicide cases that took place during spring break, investigating a murder and revealing the dark side of a holiday that should usually be a carefree time.

programme.title: Killer Grannies
programme.desc: A veteran cop falls for a beloved grandma he hopes to spend the rest of his life with, but when a home cooked meal sends them both racing to the hospital? this grannys gravy, and her sordid past, are brought under scrutiny.

programme.title: Britains Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: Our Daughter And Granddaughter Were Murdered On The Same Day
programme.desc: Friends and family of pregnant teenager Nikitta Grender talk emotionally about her murder in Newport in 2011.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: After a twenty-year-old man is crushed by a truck that fell off its jack, the victims father is brought in to face questions over whether it was really an accident... or murder.

programme.title: Killer Grannies

programme.title: The First 48
programme.desc: In Tulsa, a young mother of four is found shot on the side of the road. Detectives only clue is a photograph she took just moments before her murder.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When police find the bodies of a woman, four children and a cat, they suspect a ritualistic murder. What they later discover proves even more shocking.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When a young US Army veteran is found stabbed to death on his living room floor, suspicion lands on a neighbour who was arrested during an altercation. When Lt Kenda takes a closer look at the victims love life, he realises someone has been lying.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A grieving father asks the judge for leniency for his son whos been convicted of murdering his other son; a senior citizen spends the night in jail after continually interrupting the judge; a grieving man is removed from the courtroom.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A look at some of the wildest courtroom moments. A grieving father stares down the man who killed his daughter; a judge mutes an unruly defendant during a virtual hearing; an arsonist pleads guilty to a decades-old crime; and more.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A grieving man jumps over the gallery railing and goes after his brothers killer; a defendant gets his bond revoked after angering the judge; a man tries to blame spiked cake frosting for his failed blood alcohol test.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: After a failed plea for leniency, an angry mother flips the bird to the media after her son is sentenced; a grieving woman begs the judge to keep her brother in prison; a judge must use his mute button to silence a foul-mouthed mum.

programme.title: Killer Britain With Dermot Murnaghan
programme.desc: Kayleigh Hanks was violently strangled by her boyfriend Ian Paton at her flat in Bexhill-on-Sea, whilst their baby daughter slept in the same room.

programme.title: Killer Britain With Dermot Murnaghan
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan explores British murder stories filled with detective intrigue and chilling storylines of homicide and hate. The five murder stories are told with archived footage and key interviews from those closest to the case.

programme.title: Killer Britain With Dermot Murnaghan

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The Toyota Sera is a concept car with gull-wing doors and a 50s-style space age glass roof. Its also extremely rare, and the guys find one thats drivable, but not in the way they need.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The team acquire a Triumph Herald that has only had two owners. If the engine, brakes, and gear can be repaired, it will be a gem.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The four-wheel-drive Freelander was once Europes most popular car. It looks good and runs well, but it failed the inspection on so many points that its owner gave up.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.desc: Marks `72 Cutlass is currently languishing in storage in need of repair. Enter the `Overhaulin" guys, who soon have the car ready for the road again.

programme.title: Overhaulin
programme.desc: Got GTO? Basketball wheelchair player David has a GTO that needs a complete rebuilding. Against the odds, the Overhaulin team bounce back to slam dunk another perfect refit.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Treasure Quest: Snake Island
programme.desc: According to a legend that dates back to the 16th century, a cache of Incan gold worth hundreds of millions of dollars is hidden off the coast of southeastern Brazil. Its known as the `Treasure of the Trinity, and its eluded all who have sought it for 500 years. But a modern-day team of explorers follows a radical new theory to uncover its whereabouts: a remote, treacherous island where thousands of deadly vipers roam. Cork Graham leads the expedition to Queimada Grande - known to locals as Snake Island - armed with advanced technology to unearth complex clues and decipher puzzles that have protected the treasure for centuries. This Discovery series takes viewers along on the epic journey to one of the most dangerous places on Earth.

programme.title: It Should Happen to You
programme.desc: Gladys Glover is a small-town girl who comes to New York in search of fame and fortune. Using her savings, she rents a billboard in Columbus Circle with her name on it and, intriguing the city, becomes a TV personality. Fame brings male admirers, in the shape of Evan Adams III and Pete Sheppard, both very different types.

programme.title: Sailor of the King
programme.desc: Andrew `Canada Brown, serving in the WWII Royal Navy, is taken prisoner by the German raider that sinks his ship. The ship then puts in for repairs and Brown delays the ships departure by escaping and sniping at the sailors working on the vessel.

programme.title: Halls of Montezuma
programme.desc: In the midst of World War II, US Marines are sent on a reconnaissance patrol to a Japanese-held island in the Pacific. The mission is to track down a rocket and capture the Japanese for questioning.

programme.title: Western Union
programme.desc: Fritz Langs sprawling saga about the laying of the first transcontinental telegraph wires. Former bank robber Vance joins the Western Union telegraph company as they construct a link between Omaha and Salt Lake City.

programme.title: Ticket to Paradise
programme.desc: A divorced couple teams up and travels to Bali to stop their daughter from making the same mistake they think they made 25 years ago.

programme.title: Friends With Benefits
programme.desc: A headhunter and her latest recruit strike up a `no-strings-attached relationship, but could their casual arrangement turn into something more?

programme.desc: A highly respected CIA agent resolves to go on the run after a defector accuses her of being a spy for the Russians.

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver rustles up a show-stopping spiced cauliflower rice pie recipe as well as sharing his take on how to elevate the simple salmon fillet with his delicious Cajun-spiced po boy.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Stuffed Roast Peppers; Chicken Noodle Soup
programme.desc: Jamie shows his stuffed peppers, served on a punchy black bean stew, with avocado and feta cheese, then serves up a hearty bowl of spicy chicken noodle soup, packed full of vegetables.

programme.desc: Roasted salmon with green herbs; chicken with shallots; roasted tomatoes; sauteed sugar snap peas.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: French country omelet; veal chops with Roquefort butter and potato basil puree.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In the third series, the search for Britains best amateur baker is back as 12 passionate baking fans compete for the title. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins return as presenters, as do judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who devise the three challenges faced by the competitors each week. They incorporate a signature bake, which tests the bakers personality and creative flair; a technical bake, which tests experience; and a showstopper bake, during which the bakers are able to showcase their depth of skill and talent. Berry and Hollywood inspect, prod and sample the baked goodies as they decide who gets to have their cake - and eat it.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its the final, and the last remaining bakers are tasked with making a perfect picnic for the Signature, and for the Technical, a summer classic. And in the final Showstopper, they must create a baked celebration of our planet.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary shares her foolproof recipes for those days when you are simply run off your feet. Her mantra is "get organised, and try to do things ahead" and tonight the dishes she prepares are ones you can get together in no time. Tomato soup made from ingredients from the store cupboard, and express bacon and pesto pasta are perfect examples of dishes that take under 20 minutes to rustle up.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Durham, North Carolina, Adam competes in the Doughman, a charity relay race that gives a whole new meaning to `eat and run.

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

They incorporate a signature bake, which tests the bakers personality and creative flair; a technical bake, which tests experience; and a showstopper bake, during which the bakers are able to showcase their depth of skill and talent. Berry and Hollywood inspect, prod and sample the baked goodies as they decide who gets to have their cake - and eat it.

programme.title: James Martins French Road Trip
programme.desc: James is back in Brittany where he first learnt about French food as a student, and hes joined by fellow chef Martin Blunos for a dawn raid on the fish and vegetable market in Vannes.

programme.title: James Martins French Road Trip
programme.desc: James welcomes fellow chef Martin Blunos to his old student stamping ground in Brittany. They park up their trailer near Vannes, where the fresh seafood in the old market catches their imagination. Loaded up with a fabulous trawl they head back to the campsite to cook up Pot Au Feu and cod cheeks in local beer batter with home made tartare sauce.

programme.title: James Martins French Road Trip
programme.desc: James is back in Pornic, the Brittany seaside town where he learnt to be a French chef, and then as now the summer means a flood of delicious local strawberries.

programme.title: James Martins French Road Trip
programme.desc: After shopping in a market in Saint-Servan, near St Malo, James prepares veal with rosemary and garlic, and cauliflower cooked with local cheese.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers take the country roads to Nashville, for some down-home food and music. They cook biscuits `n gravy and visit the Grand Ole Opry stage.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree reveals her favourite shortcuts as she shares last-minute food ideas for the holidays. She uses refrigerated bread dough for a yummy Christmas tree pizza!

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond gathers her friends for a New Years brunch, featuring a menu of revamped favourites. In a fun twist on a classic, she makes everyone a baked bagel egg-in-a-hole topped with smoked salmon and more.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.desc: Jamie begins with a dish served with baked beans, and Buddy demonstrates some ways to add additional flavour to popcorn, and then Jamie shows a different way to serve avocado.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Chorizo Prawn Bake, Chocolate-Dipped Strawberries, Frittata
programme.desc: Jamie makes a rice dish with chorizo and prawns, Buddy shows his delicious chocolate-dipped strawberries and cherries, and Jamie tops it off with Indian-inspired frittata.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Seafood chowder; perfect burger; salad and fries makeover.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Chocolate chunk blondies and cookies; animal cookies.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In the third series, the search for Britains best amateur baker is back as 12 passionate baking fans compete for the title. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins return as presenters, as do judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who devise the three challenges faced by the competitors each week. They incorporate a signature bake, which tests the bakers personality and creative flair; a technical bake, which tests experience; and a showstopper bake, during which the bakers are able to showcase their depth of skill and talent. Berry and Hollywood inspect, prod and sample the baked goodies as they decide who gets to have their cake - and eat it.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In the third series, the search for Britains best amateur baker is back as 12 passionate baking fans compete for the title. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins return as presenters, as do judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who devise the three challenges faced by the competitors each week. They incorporate a signature bake, which tests the bakers personality and creative flair; a technical bake, which tests experience; and a showstopper bake, during which the bakers are able to showcase their depth of skill and talent. Berry and Hollywood inspect, prod and sample the baked goodies as they decide who gets to have their cake - and eat it.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul is in Warsaw, discovering a cafe scene at Odette that would out-do even the best Paris can offer. With head chef Jarek Nowakowski, Paul creates a new take on the Polish babka, a sponge flavoured with citrus, honey and coconut.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul is in Americas original seaside resort of Miami. Here he gets the chance to create his own version of the classic favourite, Florida key lime pie, using a delicious but deceptively simple recipe.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Charleston, South Carolina, Adam gets a big serving of Southern charm and an even bigger helping of low country cuisine.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Caseys culinary adventures take him to Wilmington, North Carolina where he tries a BBQ bonanza, a burrito and takes part in the undefeated Pierogi team challenge.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Mediterranean spot in Tennessee, a cafe in Minnesota with wild rice French toast, and has turkey in Kentucky.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: Triple D Nation: From Fried to Sides
programme.desc: Guy Fieris throwin everything in the fryer! He hits up a joint in Knoxville, TN, where the food comes straight to your car, samples spiced up crab cakes in Somers Point, NJ; crispy pig tails and huge hot dogs at a pub in Blue Ash, OH.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Cassata, Cheesecakes and Crates of Wine
programme.desc: The Valastros spend the last leg of their Italian vacation in Sicily where they visit a local winery and Buddy decides to make a Wine Bottle Cake for the owner.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Its Christmas Eve - the busiest day of the year at Carlos and all hands are on deck to make sure the customers get what they need in time for Christmas, be it cakes, cookies or pies.
