programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A bull terrier puppy needs a 3D printed implant for a new lease of life. Meanwhile, a Siamese cat has multiple injuries after being hit by a van and a beloved collie cross needs a new hip.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Following the `Bionic Vet, Noel Fitzpatrick, and his team as they use their pioneering techniques to attempt to cure pets across the country that might otherwise be beyond saving.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team enlist the help of the local community in Aberystwyth to renovate a home for a builder with a brain tumour. Michael Saunders built an extension onto his house when his son- in-laws MS meant he had to start using a wheelchair. When Michael discovered he had cancer, he was unable to finish his own home and he and his partner were left living in a cold and draughty shell of a house.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team enlist the help of the local community in Durham to reconfigure a home and reunite a family torn apart by illness. Sixteen-year-old Kieron Purvis collapsed with an undiagnosed heart condition in December 2009. Following a heart transplant and a double amputation, the Purvis family found themselves living apart because their home was not equipped for Kierons special needs.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Go behind-the-scenes of HART, the Yorkshire Ambulance Services Hazardous Area Response Team, one of 15 specialist medical units set up after the 7/7 London bombings to treat badly injured patients in extreme environments.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Behind Bars
programme.desc: Louis Theroux travels to California to spend two weeks in the notorious San Quentin State Prison. Built in 1852, San Quentin is dangerously overcrowded. Louis discovers the prisoners inner culture, intrigued by its rules and language. He meets David Silva, who is serving 521 years and 11 life sentences and is locked down for 23 hours a day, and Deborah and Rob, a transgender couple who live together inside.

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In this groundbreaking and new documentary format, Piers Morgan ventures behind bars for an encounter with diagnosed psychopath Paris Bennett, a man who is more intelligent, more cunning and more manipulative than any criminal Piers has met before.

programme.title: Nurses Down Under
programme.desc: Witness medical dramas through the eyes of the nurses, many from the UK, who work in three of Sydneys busiest hospitals.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Johannesburg
programme.desc: Louis Theroux travels to Johannesburg, where the residents find themselves increasingly besieged by crime. Despairing of the capability of the police and the courts to protect them, many have turned to an industry of private security offering protection for a price. Are the sometimes brutal methods of these private police really a solution or just another part of the problem?

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
Back From the Dead: Who Kidnapped Me?
programme.desc: Delimar Vera wants to know who helped kidnap her as a baby, and a search for answers leads to an emotional reunion, new revelations and a confrontation with her father.

programme.title: Back From the Dead: Who Kidnapped Me?

programme.desc: Katie was separated from her boyfriend when she discovered her pregnancy but reunited at the first scan. Nicole is admitted with a significant bleed, and Jolanta is hoping her third child will be born underwater.

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Two couples prove that unplanned pregnancies can happen at very different ages. Andy, whos 41, and Nina, whos 39, thought theyd long left nappies and sleepless nights behind. Jonathan, whos 21, met 18-year-old Laura when he randomly sent her a message on a social networking site. Three months later she fell pregnant.

programme.title: Nurses Down Under
programme.desc: Witness medical dramas through the eyes of the nurses, many from the UK, who work in three of Sydneys busiest hospitals.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Philadelphia
programme.desc: Louis Theroux joins the Philadelphia Police Department patrolling the most dangerous part of one of the most violent cities in America. Louis senses a society trying to contain a lawlessness born out of poverty and disaffection. Over time he sees a complex picture evolve - there are cops and robbers, guns and violence, but also an understanding between both sides.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler had been chancellor of Germany for just under a year and it was a challenging balancing act. On the one hand the Nazis had to be be mindful of president Paul von Hindenburg and the vice chancellor Franz von Papen, who were members of Germanys traditional aristocratic and ruling elite.Then, there was the Nazis own power base, the stormtroopers, made up of millions of angry, disenfranchised men who wreaked havoc on the streets of Germany.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team are at historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, commemorating 1,000 years of worship on the site. Alastair Dickenson is impressed by a tankard dating back to 1703 and Adam Schoon shows how a hidden lock in a 1640s iron chest would deter thieves.

programme.title: Yeh Dillagi

programme.title: Chandni
programme.desc: Rohit and Chandni become engaged in spite of their families refusal, based on their class differences, but when Rohit loses the function of his legs in a road accident, he leaves Chandni rather than placing a burden on her. Years later, Rohit has made a full recovery, and sets out to find Chandni, but when he does, she is engaged to another man.
